title: Strengthening SCADA System Security Against Cyber Attacks: Leveraging Machine
author: Lumbhani, Vishal
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Cybersecurity in Operational Technology (OT) environments-such as manufacturing, utilities, transportation, and critical infrastructure-aims to ensure high accuracy and visibility. As OT systems integrate with the internet through Information Technology (IT) networks, they become more vulnerable to cyber threats. Securing these systems requires both automated detection tools and human analysts to identify hidden vulnerabilities and sophisticated attacks (Cibaku, 2024). Traditional IT security approaches are often insufficient for OT and SCADA systems (Alanazi, 2023). Research shows that ensemble machine learning models perform better in detecting anomalies but may struggle with specific class label predictions (Lal, 2023). This study focuses on strengthening detection and prevention mechanisms, particularly for attacks targeting the Modbus communication protocol used in OT systems. Machine learning techniques have been widely applied in anomaly detection, but achieving high accuracy remains a challenge. By experimenting with two unsupervised ML algorithms Isolation Forest and On-Class SVM in a simulated environment, this research evaluates their performance based on Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score area. Results suggest that placing security monitoring services close to OT systems enhances visibility and control. Future studies should further validate these findings with diverse datasets to improve their real-world applicability. 
 …
